response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience, but the resume shows minimal Java/Kotlin/Spring/JVM depth — Java appears only briefly in roles from ~2014-2018 (LabWindows/CVI was C/C++, robotic arms used DLang and Java). Core stack expertise leans heavily toward TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/mobile. There is no demonstrated experience with IDE internals, language tooling, Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate, or large-scale JVM systems, which are the central requirements of this role.
strengths: Long, varied engineering background with strong craftsmanship habits (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Prior C/C++ work on a commercial IDE (NI LabWindows/CVI) shows some exposure to developer-tooling internals
Has shipped and maintained complex systems across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backends
concerns: No recent or substantial Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience listed
Kotlin is not mentioned anywhere on the resume
Primary recent stacks (TypeScript/Node, DLang) do not align with deep JVM/enterprise-Java tooling work

key_factors.factor: Prior IDE/C++ tooling work at National Instruments
